title: BLS Sequence
subtitle: Commit to the full BLS sequence before seeing the reveal.

cases.prompt: Adult found unresponsive and not breathing normally. AED is available with a two-person crew.
cases.hint: Start with responsiveness and breathing, activate help/AED, then compressions and AED cycle.

steps.label: Check breathing and pulse no more than 10 sec
Shock if advised, then immediately resume CPR

cases.explanation: BLS prioritizes rapid recognition, help/AED activation, high-quality compressions, brief analysis pauses, and immediate CPR resumption after shock/no-shock decisions.

cases.prompt: Pediatric arrest with two rescuers and AED available after initial assessment.
cases.hint: For a two-rescuer pediatric arrest, the compression-to-ventilation ratio differs from adult CPR.

steps.label: Check breathing and pulse no more than 10 sec
Pause for AED analysis when prompted
Shock if advised, then resume 15:2 CPR

cases.explanation: With two rescuers, pediatric CPR uses 15:2 until an advanced airway changes the ventilation strategy. AED analysis pauses should remain brief.

cases.prompt: Witnessed collapse with AED already beside the patient. A second rescuer can start compressions while pads are applied.
cases.hint: AED application and compressions can happen in parallel with multiple rescuers, but analysis requires stopping motion.

steps.label: Check pulse no more than 10 sec
Start CPR while pads are applied
Shock if advised or resume CPR if no shock
Continue CPR until next AED analysis cycle

cases.explanation: AED-first availability does not remove the need for pulse/breathing check and high-quality CPR. Stop motion only for analysis and shock delivery.
